data.title: Refactor sprint-list-epics.sh to use ticket list-epics
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor `sprint-list-epics.sh` to call `ticket list-epics` instead of directly invoking the ticket reducer. Preserve the tab-separated output format with the blocker_ids field and the three-valued exit code contract. Add to boundary hook allowlist if the script becomes a thin wrapper.

**Why**: Moves the epic-listing logic into the ticket CLI boundary. Eliminates a direct reducer caller.

**Scope**:
- IN: sprint-list-epics.sh refactor to use `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list-epics` (via shim), preserve tab-separated output with blocker_ids, preserve three-valued exit code (0=ready, 1=no epics, 2=all blocked), preserve --max-children / --min-children / --all flag support, existing test suite passes, boundary-hook allowlist entry with ticket citation
- OUT: Changes to `ticket list-epics` itself (epic 2c28-e5b9 scope); refactoring callers of sprint-list-epics.sh (the wrapper preserves the contract, callers unchanged)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint-list-epics.sh invokes `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list-epics` and preserves the tab-separated output format (including P* in-progress marker, BLOCKING column, P0 bug header) and the three-valued exit code contract
  ← Satisfies: "sprint-list-epics.sh calls ticket list-epics"
- When this story is complete, sprint-list-epics.sh's existing test suite passes with no behavioral regression
  ← Satisfies: "Each refactored script passes its existing test suite with no behavioral regression"
- When this story is complete, sprint-list-epics.sh is added to the boundary hook allowlist with an inline comment citing epic e1b2-2653
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] sprint/SKILL.md Phase 1 depends on P* / BLOCKING / P0-header / three-valued exit-code behaviors — preservation is a hard requirement
- [Dependency] Depends on `ticket list-epics` from epic 2c28-e5b9 (inter-epic)
- [Testing] Diff output on the same fixture before/after; expect byte-for-byte equivalence

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
